data.epi_content:
"I heard you slapped Vivian today.

I've been letting you do whatever you want because you haven't crossed the line until now..."

Evan speaks with an uncharacteristically lowered voice, acting as if he's angry.

I cut him off because I didn't want to hear it.

Besides, even though he was angry, he didn't look very intimidating since he was just a bookworm who knew nothing but magic.

"Yes. Today. Was it today?

Just come in, don't lurk awkwardly at the door like you have no manners."

I wonder what made him so angry about the word "lurking."

The vein on Evan's temple visibly throbbed.

Still, perhaps conscious of my comment about manners, he gave a slight bow when I opened the door and came inside.

I took out a chair meant for guests and placed a cup on the table.

I put an appropriate amount of tea leaves in a white infuser... oh, I had tea.

It's interesting how my hands move habitually.

Anyway, after placing it in the cup, I created hot water with magic and poured it in.

A refreshing herbal scent spread as the blue color diffused into the bland water.

I wonder if I looked like this too.

The white infuser clinging to the tea leaves, and the vibrant green leaves losing their lively color, turning dark and dull.

"Drink it. I served it because you're a guest, after all."

"Do you think I came here because I wanted to drink tea?"

"When a guest enters a room with the host's consent and the host offers something, it's common courtesy for the guest to accept it with a smile and gratitude."

"Well said. And is it also courtesy to slap a commoner you dislike?"

"Strictly speaking, she's not a commoner. She's a knight's daughter."

"That's not the point. What matters is that you looked down on Vivian because of your status."

The issue would be that I touched her first.

Touching someone's body without consent is beyond rude, and if the genders were reversed, it would be a crime.

I remembered saying this before but didn't bother to mention it aloud this time.

Since I've already experienced this once, I don't think I'll have the same heated argument as before.

Evan probably feels differently, though.

"So, why did you come here?

To scold me? To say how could I do that to Vivian?"

"Rather than scolding, I came to reproach you.

No matter what I say, you won't admit your mistakes or try to change your attitude."

Why should I?

There's no reason or justification.

Strangely, even though this is the second time I'm experiencing this situation, the conversation doesn't seem to be changing.

Then perhaps everything that happens from now on will be the same too.

Does that mean I'll be trapped in this time loop forever?

I can't even escape.

Then is a decent cup of tea and a piece of expensive chocolate that's honestly just bitter enough... no, it's not enough.

"I suppose so."

I pick up the cup and take a sip of tea.

The warm feeling made me feel a bit more at ease.

I won't change myself, or rather, I can't.

Innate compulsions and learned behaviors have turned not just my body but my mind into a machine.

Considering that I was once a sturdy man who never learned manners, the obsession and compulsion ingrained in this young lady's body and mind are tremendous.

"Even so, is there really a need to reproach me? While hurting each other's feelings?"

"Right, that's what Duke Mecklenburg always used to say.

Why bother with those beneath us, why control corrupt merchants, things like that.

Even when it was obvious the family would be in danger, he'd say the same things. You really take after your father."
data.epi_content2:
"My father is a rational man, while I'm apparently so irrational that I slapped Vivian, whom you like, and hurt your feelings too."

So personal attacks are best when they insult parents and family honor.

He's probably already passed away by now, haha.

"Rational? No, he's just so conservative that he refuses to change anything.

That's why that incident happened at the mine."

Despite asking him not to cross the line and to limit his insults to me, Evan pays no attention.

Evan easily tramples over clearly drawn lines.

We built years of friendship, or trust, or perhaps love—though that might have been just my delusion. Was it all just my imagination?

Or maybe he's just blinded by love.

"Why, are you planning to marry me?

If you love Vivian so much, you should marry her.

If not, please stay out of our family's business."

At those words, Evan let out a scoffing sound and continued.

"How can I stay out of it when we're so entangled in many ways?"

"I suppose so. You still haven't disbanded that money-draining knight order that serves no purpose.

The poor people of your territory are breaking their backs to maintain that useless circus.

If trade gets cut off, you'll have to disband that circus too."

Evan gritted his teeth.

He glared at me as if he might get up and strangle me at any moment.

Still, it doesn't escalate like it did before.

It's better to sit here than to shout at each other, slap faces, and burst into tears.

Let's just calmly strangle each other with words.

"I know the problems.

But it's about not forgetting our roots and maintaining our honor.

If the problems grow, we can simply reduce the scale."

"You're no different from Duke Strelitz. He also claims to understand the problems."

"Don't compare me to my father."

Evan hates his father.

When young people think of stubborn old men, Evan thinks of his father.

"I'm not comparing. I'm saying you act like him."

Silence fell.

I closed my eyes slightly.

Evan clenched his fist as if trying to suppress his anger, then wiped his forehead with a handkerchief from his pocket.

Despite this, his face was flushed, and he seemed to be trembling slightly from the blood rushing to his head.

After downing a cup of tea, he spoke again.

"It might be in your blood. You're following in the footsteps of your father, whom you used to complain about.

Looking down on others, forming cliques, creating unnecessary conflicts."

With an expression that suggested I was hearing nonsense, I brewed more tea for Evan's empty cup.

"I love my father. He's a good person. But I don't know about following in his footsteps."

"Why, was he too disgusted to touch a commoner's cheek?"

Thinking about my father's personality, that might be true.

He doesn't like it when you approach him, not just commoners.

He's so obsessed with cleanliness that I'd almost call him a patient.

"About the slap... Vivian grabbed me first, but I guess that wasn't important to you."

The angry man in front of me frowned at my muttered words.

"If you have something to say, say it clearly."

"Instead of words like 'reproach,' why not use a simple expression like 'came to torment me'?

No matter what happens between us, we both know why these things happen and why we can't solve the problems.

This is just, this is just burning through emotions. Like searching for useless roots and honor."
data.epi_content3:
"How can you say that? You know very well why our family is in this situation."

"I know well, since I almost had that useless family name attached to mine.

You look like your beautiful late mother. That's fortunate. And you also resemble your father, and sometimes even my father.

Growing up, whenever I met you, I used to think: you might be a terrible amalgamation of the worst traits of those three people."

Hearing that, Evan's face turned red and blue.

Then his voice rose.

"It must have been hard spending years with someone who gathered all those flaws?"

"Of course it was hard. At first, I only met you because my father told me to."

"Right, your family is so great. So great that your brother is waiting for his head to fall off in prison."

When I heard this before, I cried and fought with Evan, shouting.

I didn't know exactly what had happened, only that my brother was imprisoned, so I was terribly anxious.

And hearing Evan's words, it felt like the emotional line I was holding onto had snapped.

Hearing it for the second time, I feel empty rather than angry.

I feel dazed.

It's like my focused pupils are dilating.

My vision blurred.

Looking vaguely in Evan's direction, I spoke.

"Is that so? I don't know.

So, Evan, why don't you stop wasting time and leave?"

"Erica. I came because you slapped Vivian.

If you had just apologized to Vivian for slapping her damn cheek, we wouldn't be saying all this nonsense to each other.

The three of us used to get along so well at first. What's the problem? Why did you change so much?"

Change?

My goodness, how surprising.

What changed?

I think Evan changed first.

Is he so in love with Vivian that he doesn't care about anything else around her?

"Stop pretending you don't know."

"..."

"You know very well what the problem is.

Just, just. I'm the problem.

I'm tired of it all. Don't worry, it won't become an issue between our families.

It's all over. It's all over, there's nothing left.

I have nothing left, and you just need to hold hands with Vivian and spend happy times together.

You still have so much."

Evan probably doesn't know that my family has completely fallen apart by now.

He'll just dismiss it as the nonsensical ramblings of an emotional girl.

I hope he does.

Soon I'll be isolated in the classroom.

The children who used to flatter me and rub their hands together will now spit trash and saliva at me.

No will or reason to live on...

"No reason, huh."

Evan flinches as he looks at me.

"Erica, about your family, I'm sor—"

"Evan, leave. Being in a girl's dormitory in the middle of the night wouldn't be good for your reputation.

We said some things that went too far today. I'm sorry for bringing up family. I'm sorry for saying harsh things. I'll try to apologize to Vivian if possible.

So please... *sigh*... leave."

He looked at me for a long time with a complicated expression, perhaps feeling guilty, then bit his lip once and stomped out of the room.

As I was clearing away the teacup and chocolate on the table, I caught a glimpse of myself in the mirror—an expressionless girl with tears streaming down her face.

With a heavy feeling, I ran my palm over my face.

The tears had stopped.
